<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer v1.2.12 -->

# src/implement

Implements the `are implement` command — executes AI-driven code changes in isolated worktrees with and without ARE documentation, evaluates quality via blind comparison, and persists metrics.

## Contents

**[executor.ts](./executor.ts)** — `executeImplementation` calls aiService.call with buildImplementationPrompt output, allowedTools: 'Read,Glob,Grep,Bash,Write,Edit,Task,WebSearch,WebFetch,TodoWrite', maxTurns: 100, 40-minute timeout, extracts metrics via extractImplementationMetrics (with baseRef support for cumulative change measurement), computes cost via getModelPricing/computeCost, returns ImplementationRunResult with token counts, latency, cost breakdown, git stats, test/build/lint results.

**[evaluator.ts](./evaluator.ts)** — `evaluateImplementations` runs blind AI evaluation with randomized planA/planB presentation (50/50 via Math.random()) to prevent position bias, scores implementations on five weighted criteria (codeQuality 30%, completeness 25%, testCoverage 20%, errorHandling 15%, adherenceToSpec 10%), calls aiService.call with maxTurns: 1, no allowedTools (non-agentic), parses JSON response via extractJson regex (`/```(?:json)?\s*\n([\s\S]*?)\n```/`), maps scores back to withDocs/withoutDocs labels, computes weighted totals, returns ImplementationEvaluation with totalScoreA/totalScoreB.

**[metrics.ts](./metrics.ts)** — `extractImplementationMetrics` analyzes worktree git stats (filesCreated, filesModified, linesAdded, linesDeleted, commitCount) via simpleGit().diffSummary([baseRef, 'HEAD']) when baseRef provided (SHA before AI run), legacy fallback to .diffSummary(['HEAD~1']) or .status() when absent, optionally runs test suite (parses pass/fail counts via `/(\d+)\s+pass(?:ed|ing)/i`, `/(\d+)\s+fail(?:ed|ing)/i` from npm test output), linter (extracts errors/warnings via `/(\d+)\s+error/i`, `/(\d+)\s+warning/i` from npm run lint), build (checks exit code from npm run build), returns ImplementationMetrics with all quality gate results.

**[prompts.ts](./prompts.ts)** — `buildImplementationPrompt` constructs agentic prompt with task, optional plan text (from `are plan`), conditional quality gates (runTests: "Write comprehensive tests for all new code", runBuild: "Ensure the build passes (run `npm run build`)", runLint: "Fix all lint errors and warnings"), exploration directive when no plan provided; `buildEvaluatorPrompt` constructs blind evaluation prompt with strict JSON schema enforcing planA/planB objects with score (1-5) and reasoning fields, plus top-level summary field.

**[storage.ts](./storage.ts)** — Persists ImplementationComparison records to `.agents-reverse-engineer/implementations/<id>/` containing `comparison.json`, `implementation-with-docs.log`, `implementation-without-docs.log`; exports `saveComparison` (writes directory structure), `loadComparison` (exact or partial ID match), `listComparisons` (sorted by startTime descending).

**[types.ts](./types.ts)** — Defines data contracts: ImplementationMetrics (git stats + test/build/lint results), ImplementationRunResult (tokens, latency, cost, metrics, log, success/error), CriterionScore (1-5 score + reasoning), ImplementationEvaluation (randomized planA/planB labels, five criterion scores, totals, summary), ComparisonDeltas (computed differences for cost, latency, file ratios, quality scores), ImplementationComparison (full comparison record with branches, results, evaluation, deltas), ImplementOptions (CLI flags).

**[index.ts](./index.ts)** — Re-exports public API: executeImplementation, extractImplementationMetrics, buildImplementationPrompt, buildEvaluatorPrompt, evaluateImplementations, saveComparison, loadComparison, listComparisons, renderComparison, renderHeader, renderPhaseStart, renderPhaseComplete, renderList, plus all types.

## Subdirectories

**[views/](./views/)** — Renders terminal UI for comparison results (side-by-side metrics table, quality scores, artifact paths) and saved comparison lists (date, task, model, cost, status columns).

## Architecture

**Two-phase execution workflow:**
1. `executeImplementation` runs twice (with and without docs) in isolated worktrees, each producing ImplementationRunResult
2. `evaluateImplementations` performs blind quality assessment with randomized presentation order
3. Results combined into ImplementationComparison, deltas computed, persisted via `saveComparison`

**AI service integration:** All AI interactions delegated to AIService layer (subprocess management, retry logic, telemetry). Implementation runs use taskLabel: `implement:${cwd.includes('without-docs') ? 'without-docs' : 'with-docs'}`; evaluation uses taskLabel: 'implement:evaluation'.

**Metric extraction:** Git analysis via simpleGit with baseRef support for cumulative change measurement, quality checks via npm script subprocess invocations (test/lint/build) using runSubprocess from ../ai/subprocess.js, all failures caught with zero/false defaults.

## Behavioral Contracts

**Evaluation weights:** `{codeQuality: 0.30, completeness: 0.25, testCoverage: 0.20, errorHandling: 0.15, adherenceToSpec: 0.10}`

**Timeouts:** `IMPLEMENTATION_TIMEOUT_MS = 2_400_000` (40 min), `EVAL_TIMEOUT_MS = 300_000` (5 min)

**Allowed tools:** Implementation: `Read,Glob,Grep,Bash,Write,Edit,Task,WebSearch,WebFetch,TodoWrite`; Evaluation: empty string (text-only)

**JSON extraction regex:** `/```(?:json)?\s*\n([\s\S]*?)\n```/` for markdown code fences, fallback to indexOf('{') and lastIndexOf('}') for raw objects

**Test output parsing:** `/(\d+)\s+pass(?:ed|ing)/i`, `/(\d+)\s+fail(?:ed|ing)/i` (Vitest/Jest)

**Lint output parsing:** `/(\d+)\s+error/i`, `/(\d+)\s+warning/i`

**Quality check timeouts:** test=300s, lint=120s, build=300s

**Score range:** Evaluation criteria use 1-5 scale (CriterionScore interface)

## Integration Points

Depends on:
- `../ai/service.js` → AIService (subprocess orchestration, retry logic, telemetry)
- `../ai/subprocess.js` → runSubprocess (spawn npm scripts with timeoutMs/cwd)
- `../dashboard/cost-calculator.js` → getModelPricing, computeCost, CostBreakdown type

Consumed by:
- `../cli/implement.ts` — wires up worktree creation, branch switching, subprocess invocations
